_comment: v6 (2026-04-26): Pilot v9 vs v6 RCA showed v6's bias priors v3 yields best 14-way (48.2%) and crime-only (42.5%); v9 with priors v5 + retrained MIL got 37.5% / 27.5% — better non-crime FP control (8.3%) but worse fine-grained classification. Plan B: revert to v3 priors but keep v9's pipeline improvements (BCE-trained MIL, priming guard 0.5, GAP_HARD 0.5, MIL_CONF_MIN 0.4). Goal: combine v6's classification accuracy with v9's anomaly detection improvements.
